article_meta.title: 4 Republicans defy Speaker Johnson to force House vote on extending ACA subsidies The Associated PressAP17 Dec 2025 Four

reasoning: The model makes several errors: it uses non-standard bias type labels ('Labeling Bias', 'Selection and Omission Bias', 'Spin/Word Choice Bias', 'Source Selection Bias') instead of the defined taxonomy. It has one clear attribution rule violation - flagging 'assailed the subsidies as propping up a failed marketplace' which is attributed language from the conservative wing, not the author's own characterization. Some flagged items are defensible (e.g., 'stunning move', 'soaring') but are mixed with questionable ones. The 'Source Selection Bias' item is not a direct text quote but a structural observation, which is somewhat valid but uses invented bias type. The model misses some instances while catching others.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The third flagged item includes 'assailed the subsidies as propping up a failed marketplace' which is language attributed to the conservative wing of the party. The word 'assailed' is the author's verb choice and could be flagged, but 'propping up a failed marketplace' is attributed to the conservatives. This is a partial attribution violation. The fourth item fabricates a quote that doesn't exist in the text verbatim.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: The 'centrist Republicans' flag is somewhat defensible but also questionable - it's a common descriptor in political reporting. 'Stunning move' and 'soaring' are genuinely loaded author word choices. The source selection observation has some merit but is presented as a direct text quote when it's really a structural analysis. Mixed quality overall.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model catches 'stunning move' and 'soaring' which are genuine bias instances. It misses 'pushed ahead' (loaded verb suggesting haste), 'the magic number of 218' (informal/loaded), 'revolt' (dramatic framing), 'arduous climb' (loaded characterization), and 'an encouraging sign for Democrats' (author editorializing). Several defensible instances are missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The model uses non-standard bias type labels throughout: 'Labeling Bias', 'Selection and Omission Bias', 'Spin/Word Choice Bias', and 'Source Selection Bias' are not in the provided taxonomy. While some are close to valid types (e.g., 'Spin/Word Choice Bias' approximates 'Word Choice' or 'Spin'), none exactly match the defined list. This is a systematic labeling error.
